# Neogene Aquifer

**Summary**: Geological formation studied in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-ahmad|Ahmad, 2024]] studied the Neogene Aquifer

- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] investigated as a potential site for managed aquifer recharge.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] modeled the Neogene aquifer to study its high water salinity and limited development potential.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] investigated as a potential site for managed aquifer recharge.

- [[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995]] modeled the Neogene aquifer to study its high water salinity and limited development potential.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- studied the Neogene Aquifer ([[2024-ahmad|Ahmad, 2024]])

- Material D within this formation showed superior suitability for injection compared to shallower materials. ([[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026, p. 170]])

- The permeability ranges from 0.1 to 300 m/d, with thickness between 50 and 125 meters. ([[1995-hasan|Hasan, 1995, p. 58]])
